title: CPU throttling detected for ml-inference pod ml-inference-328-ghi
description: Pod ml-inference-328-ghi in namespace staging is being CPU throttled. CPU usage regularly exceeds limit of 4.0. Throttling rate: 64% over the last 5 minutes. P99 latency increased from 25ms to 375ms.
root_cause: CPU limits set too low for actual workload
resolution: Reduced sidecar CPU request and added resource quotas

logs: Performance degradation due to CPU limit enforcement
CPU usage 160% exceeding threshold
Warning: cpu.cfs_period_us throttling triggered

title: order-service failing: PostgreSQL max_connections reached
description: order-service is experiencing connection pool exhaustion. All 50 connections in the pool are active. 13 requests are waiting for connections. Average wait time: 4274ms. Database orders-db shows max_connections = 60 reached.
root_cause: Sudden traffic spike to service causing pool saturation
resolution: Implemented read replicas to distribute read load

logs: FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
Cannot acquire connection from pool within 5s
Timeout waiting for idle connection: pool exhausted

title: Network timeout: api-gateway cannot reach external API
description: Pod api-gateway-871-jkl in namespace production is experiencing network failures. Cannot connect to elasticsearch at 10.0.29.13:9092. Error: 'connection refused'. Packet loss: 69%. Latency: 4850ms (normal: 11ms).
root_cause: Firewall rule blocking port 9092
resolution: Restarted Istio sidecar proxy and verified Envoy configuration

logs: read tcp: connection reset by peer
connect: connection refused
net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection

title: ml-inference deployment failing: ImagePullBackOff for gcr.io/ml-inference:v3.1.1
description: Pod ml-inference-261-vwx in namespace development is stuck in ImagePullBackOff. Failed to pull image 'gcr.io/ml-inference:v3.1.1' from registry ecr.aws. Error: 'denied: requested access to the resource is denied'. The deployment was triggered 37 minutes ago.

title: CoreDNS errors affecting pod api-gateway-791-jkl in namespace monitoring
description: Pod api-gateway-791-jkl in namespace monitoring is experiencing DNS resolution failures. Unable to resolve hostname 'api-gateway.monitoring.svc.cluster.local'. nslookup returns: 'connection timed out'. CoreDNS pods in kube-system are reporting timeout errors.
root_cause: NetworkPolicy blocking DNS traffic (UDP port 53)
resolution: Fixed CoreDNS ConfigMap — added missing stub domain

logs: plugin/errors: 2 api-gateway.monitoring.svc.cluster.local. A: read udp: i/o timeout
getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN api-gateway.monitoring.svc.cluster.local
lookup api-gateway.monitoring.svc.cluster.local on 10.81.243.116: no such host

title: Out of memory: user-auth containers killed by OOM killer

resolution: Increased memory limit from 1.0 to 512Mi

title: Out of memory: user-auth containers killed by OOM killer

root_cause: Sudden traffic spike causing increased memory allocation
resolution: Added pod auto-scaling (HPA) with memory-based triggers

title: Database connection pool exhausted for order-service
description: order-service is experiencing connection pool exhaustion. All 200 connections in the pool are active. 25 requests are waiting for connections. Average wait time: 3145ms. Database cache-db shows max_connections = 200 reached.
root_cause: Network latency between application and database
resolution: Implemented read replicas to distribute read load

logs: Timeout waiting for idle connection: pool exhausted
FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
Cannot acquire connection from pool within 30s

title: Network timeout: payment-service cannot reach external API
description: Pod payment-service-295-mno in namespace staging is experiencing network failures. Cannot connect to external-api at 10.0.68.224:5672. Error: 'no route to host'. Packet loss: 55%. Latency: 2238ms (normal: 46ms).
root_cause: NetworkPolicy blocking egress traffic to required service
resolution: Set MTU to 1400 in CNI configuration to match network path

net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection

title: Service discovery broken: all-services cannot resolve hostnames

title: Connection timeout errors in order-service — pool starvation
description: order-service is experiencing connection pool exhaustion. All 100 connections in the pool are active. 9 requests are waiting for connections. Average wait time: 2678ms. Database orders-db shows max_connections = 110 reached.
root_cause: Network latency between application and database
resolution: Fixed connection leak by ensuring connections closed in finally blocks

logs: Timeout waiting for idle connection: pool exhausted
FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
Connection pool size 100 exceeded for orders-db

title: Network connectivity loss in external-adapter pod external-adapter-915-stu
description: Pod external-adapter-915-stu in namespace monitoring is experiencing network failures. Cannot connect to rabbitmq at 10.0.14.75:5672. Error: 'no route to host'. Packet loss: 32%. Latency: 3850ms (normal: 4ms).
root_cause: NetworkPolicy blocking egress traffic to required service
resolution: Set MTU to 1400 in CNI configuration to match network path

logs: read tcp: connection reset by peer
dial tcp 10.0.14.75:5672: i/o timeout
net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection

title: user-service failing: PostgreSQL max_connections reached
description: user-service is experiencing connection pool exhaustion. All 20 connections in the pool are active. 26 requests are waiting for connections. Average wait time: 1184ms. Database users-db shows max_connections = 25 reached.
root_cause: Pool size misconfiguration — pool too small for workload
resolution: Added PgBouncer connection pooler in front of PostgreSQL

logs: FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
Cannot acquire connection from pool within 30s
Connection pool size 20 exceeded for users-db

root_cause: NetworkPolicy blocking egress traffic to required service
resolution: Set MTU to 1400 in CNI configuration to match network path

title: DNS resolution failures in api-gateway pod api-gateway-512-ghi

root_cause: CoreDNS ConfigMap misconfiguration
resolution: Set ndots:2 in pod spec to reduce DNS query volume

title: Network connectivity loss in payment-service pod payment-service-948-def
description: Pod payment-service-948-def in namespace production is experiencing network failures. Cannot connect to external-api at 10.0.119.122:9200. Error: 'no route to host'. Packet loss: 50%. Latency: 735ms (normal: 49ms).
root_cause: Service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) sidecar proxy misconfiguration
resolution: Added firewall rule allowing port 9200 outbound

logs: net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection

read tcp: connection reset by peer

title: Connection timeout errors in inventory-api — pool starvation
description: inventory-api is experiencing connection pool exhaustion. All 200 connections in the pool are active. 46 requests are waiting for connections. Average wait time: 2923ms. Database postgres-prod shows max_connections = 205 reached.
root_cause: Slow database queries causing connections to be held open
resolution: Increased connection pool size from 200 to 600

logs: Cannot acquire connection from pool within 30s
Timeout waiting for idle connection: pool exhausted
FATAL: sorry, too many clients already

resolution: Tuned JVM GC settings (G1GC, MaxGCPauseMillis=200)

resolution: Configured HPA with CPU utilization target of 70%

root_cause: Pool size misconfiguration — pool too small for workload
resolution: Implemented read replicas to distribute read load

logs: FATAL: sorry, too many clients already

title: Database connection pool exhausted for user-service
description: user-service is experiencing connection pool exhaustion. All 20 connections in the pool are active. 35 requests are waiting for connections. Average wait time: 3308ms. Database orders-db shows max_connections = 25 reached.
root_cause: Slow database queries causing connections to be held open
resolution: Added PgBouncer connection pooler in front of PostgreSQL

logs: Cannot acquire connection from pool within 60s

title: Packet loss between api-gateway and elasticsearch

root_cause: Service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) sidecar proxy misconfiguration
resolution: Updated NetworkPolicy to allow egress to 10.0.115.58:9200

dial tcp 10.0.115.58:9200: i/o timeout
net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection

title: Service discovery broken: service-mesh cannot resolve hostnames
description: Pod service-mesh-655-def in namespace production is experiencing DNS resolution failures. Unable to resolve hostname 'service-mesh.production.svc.cluster.local'. nslookup returns: 'no such host'. CoreDNS pods in kube-system are reporting timeout errors.
root_cause: Node-level DNS cache corruption
resolution: Scaled CoreDNS deployment to 5 replicas

title: Memory limit exceeded: user-auth pod user-auth-354-mno terminated

root_cause: Third-party library memory leak under concurrent requests
resolution: Added pod auto-scaling (HPA) with memory-based triggers

Log: dial tcp <IP>:9092: i/o timeout
Log: net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection
Root Cause: Firewall rule blocking port 9092
Resolution: Set MTU to 1400 in CNI configuration to match network path
Affected Services: external-adapter, payment-service, notification-svc

A: read udp: i/o timeout Root Cause: NetworkPolicy blocking DNS traffic UDP port 53 Resolution: Fixed CoreDNS ConfigMap added missing stub domain Affected Services: service-mesh, all-services

title: Packet loss between notification-svc and external-api
description: Pod notification-svc-661-stu in namespace kube-system is experiencing network failures. Cannot connect to external-api at 10.0.131.154:5432. Error: 'i/o timeout'. Packet loss: 12%. Latency: 2636ms (normal: 15ms).
root_cause: Service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) sidecar proxy misconfiguration
resolution: Updated NetworkPolicy to allow egress to 10.0.131.154:5432

logs: read tcp: connection reset by peer
connect: connection refused
net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection
